intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant 'Willwebera montana'. However, I did find that 'Willwebera' is a genus of flowering plants in the family Gunneraceae, commonly known as the 'water wrens'. 

If you are referring to Willwebera or its close relatives in Gunneraceae, then here is a paragraph about the Gunnera genus:

The Gunnera genus, which includes the plant Gunnera manicata, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant with large leaves and small white or yellow flowers. The health benefits of Gunnera include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Gunnera include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gunnera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and digestive issues.
